A current of $5.2~\text{A}$ is passed through a solution containing $\text{Cu}^{2+}$ ions for $22~\text{minutes}$. Calculate the mass of $\text{Cu}$ deposited at the cathode. ($M_\text{Cu} = 63.5~\text{g/mol}$, $F = 96485~\text{C/mol}$)
explanation: $Q = It = 6866.43~\text{C}$. $n_e = \frac{Q}{F} = 0.0712~\text{mol}$. The ion requires $2~\text{mol}$ of electrons per mole of metal, so $n_{\text{metal}} = \frac{0.0712}{2} = 0.0356~\text{mol}$. Mass = $n \times M = (0.0356)(63.5) \approx 2.26~\text{g}$.

explanation: $Q = It = 6411.82~\text{C}$. $n_e = \frac{Q}{F} = 0.0665~\text{mol}$. The ion requires $2~\text{mol}$ of electrons per mole of metal, so $n_{\text{metal}} = \frac{0.0665}{2} = 0.0332~\text{mol}$. Mass = $n \times M = (0.0332)(65.4) \approx 2.17~\text{g}$.
